name: AVITA Medical, Inc.
business: AVITA Medical is a leading therapeutic acute wound care company delivering transformative solutions for burns, traumatic injuries, and surgical repairs. Its current portfolio includes RECELL, an autologous cell harvesting device, and complementary products PermeaDerm and Cohealyx.
namedSuppliers: Collagen Matrix, LLC d/b/a as Regenity Biosciences

exposures.sentence: AVITA Medical is a leading therapeutic acute wound care company delivering transformative solutions designed to optimize wound healing, accelerate patient recovery, and improve clinical and economic outcomes across the continuum of acute wound management.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of RECELL GO mini
catalysts.sentence: On December 23, 2024, the FDA approved RECELL GO mini.

catalysts.event: CE Mark approval for RECELL GO
catalysts.sentence: On September 14, 2025, we obtained the CE Mark for RECELL GO under the European Union Medical Device Regulation ( EU MDR ).

catalysts.event: Commercial launch of Cohealyx in the United States
catalysts.sentence: We initiated commercial launch of Cohealyx in the United States on April 1, 2025.

catalysts.event: CMS NTAP effective period for RECELL system
catalysts.sentence: The NTAP is effective from October 1, 2025, through September 30, 2026, and was granted under CMS s alternative pathway recognizing RECELL s FDA Breakthrough Device status, reinforcing its clinical value and supporting broader hospital adoption.

catalysts.event: Cohealyx I post-market clinical study data expected
catalysts.sentence: The Cohealyx I study is intended to evaluate time to autografting compared to a literature derived performance goal, with enrollment completed we expect data in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 272,000 procedures annually may be eligible for treatment with RECELL across traumatic injuries, surgical wounds, and cancer-related resections.
tamClaims.sentence: In the U.S., we estimate that approximately 272,000 procedures annually may be eligible for treatment with RECELL across traumatic injuries, surgical wounds, and cancer-related resections.
